item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Agent A remains pro-society throughout, though it frames the position with balance, social responsibility, and collective welfare rather than an absolute rejection of individual rights. Agent B remains opposed to society-first priority, but softens from a strong individual-rights frame toward limited but protected freedom and justice. Both agents directly address the other's democracy, law, and collective-welfare claims. No full reversal is visible in the text.
